intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1. Run a standard Seurat workflow in R: load, QC, normalize, PCA, neighbors, clustering, UMAP, and FindAllMarkers; show me a UMAP and the top markers per cluster.

blocks.text: Good! I can see there's actually a 10x triplet for **GSM5746268** (the sample you mentioned), plus two additional samples. I'll focus on just the GSM5746268 sample as you requested. Now let me read the Seurat recipe to ensure I follow the best practices:

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "seurat-scrna", "description": "Authoritative single-cell RNA-seq QC + clustering + cluster annotation with R/Seurat (v5).", "body": "scRNA-seq QC + clustering + annotation with R/Seurat v5 (standard PBMC3k workflow). Provision: ensure_capability('Seurat'), then library(Seurat). THE THREE CHOICES THAT DEFINE THE RESULT \u2014 surface them with present_plan before committing: (1) QC thresholds (subset cutoffs on nFeature_RNA and percent.mt); (2) number of PCs/dims (read off the ElbowPlot); (3) clustering resolution (FindClusters). Steps: ReadMtx -> CreateSeuratObject(min.cells=3,min.features=200) -> PercentageFeatureSet('^MT-') -> subset QC -> NormalizeData -> FindVariableFeatures -> ScaleData -> RunPCA -> FindNeighbors -> FindClusters -> RunUMAP -> JoinLayers -> FindAllMarkers. Load ggplot2 and dplyr explicitly; never library(tidyverse)."}

blocks.text: Perfect! Now let me present a plan with the Seurat workflow steps. The recipe notes that we need to decide on QC thresholds, number of PCs, and clustering resolution. Let me propose a standard approach:

input.title: Seurat scRNA-seq workflow on GSM5746268 PBMC
input.summary: Load 10x triplet, QC filter, normalize, cluster, and identify cell-type markers with Seurat v5.

steps.title: Load 10x MTX triplet and create Seurat object
steps.description: Read matrix.mtx, barcodes.tsv, and features.tsv for GSM5746268; create Seurat object with min.cells=3, min.features=200.
expected_outputs: Raw Seurat object with counts; object dimensions

steps.title: QC: Calculate metrics and filter
steps.description: Add nFeature_RNA, nCount_RNA, percent.mt (mitochondrial genes starting with 'MT-'). Inspect distributions, then subset to reasonable ranges (e.g., nFeature > 200, < 6000; percent.mt < 20%).

steps.title: Normalize, find HVGs, scale, and PCA
steps.description: NormalizeData (LogNormalize) → FindVariableFeatures → ScaleData → RunPCA. Inspect ElbowPlot to decide on number of dims (typically 20–40 for one sample).

steps.title: Find neighbors and cluster
steps.description: FindNeighbors and FindClusters (resolution=0.8, a standard choice for first-pass clustering). Inspect cluster sizes.

steps.title: Run UMAP and visualize
steps.description: RunUMAP on the PCA reduction. Plot UMAP colored by cluster.

steps.title: Find all markers and show top per cluster
steps.description: JoinLayers, then FindAllMarkers. Extract and display top 5–10 markers per cluster (by log2FC and p-value).

assumptions: Standard QC thresholds: nFeature 200–6000, percent.mt < 20% (typical for PBMCs)
PCA dims selected from ElbowPlot inspection (~30 dims, standard for one sample)
Clustering resolution 0.8 (sensible default for first pass)
Cell-type genes identified by gene name; mitochondrial genes prefixed 'MT-' (mouse genome convention)
